metadata.name: Cleave — Path of Peril normal vs cleave cost
metadata.description: Tests Path of Peril's Cleave mechanic (CR 702.148) in two sequential casts. Cast 1: p1 casts Path of Peril normally ({1}{B}{B}). Condition::WasCleaved is false — the if_false branch executes, destroying only creatures with mana value <= 2 (the two Llanowar Elves). The two Bladetusk Boars (mana value 4) survive. Cast 2: p1 casts the second Path of Peril with the cleave cost ({4}{W}{B}) using the 'cast_spell_cleave' harness action. Condition::WasCleaved is true — the if_true branch (all creatures) executes, destroying the remaining Bladetusk Boars. Fixed in PB-19: path_of_peril.rs now uses DestroyAll { max_cmc: 2 } for the normal cast branch.
metadata.generation_notes: PB-19 fix: path_of_peril.rs now correctly uses DestroyAll { filter: { has_card_type: Creature, max_cmc: 2 }, cant_be_regenerated: false } for the if_false branch. Before PB-19, the if_false branch used AllPermanentsMatching(creature) which incorrectly destroyed all creatures regardless of mana value. The fix was made possible by TargetFilter.max_cmc (added in PB-17) and Effect::DestroyAll (added in PB-19). Updated assertions to reflect correct behavior: Bladetusk Boar (mana value 4 > 2) survives the normal cast; only Llanowar Elves (mana value 1 <= 2) die. The cleave cast then destroys the remaining Bladetusk Boars.

script.step_note: Cast 1: p1 casts Path of Peril normally ({1}{B}{B}) without cleave. Condition::WasCleaved is false — the if_false branch executes: DestroyAll { creature, max_cmc: 2 }. Only Llanowar Elves (mana value 1 <= 2) are destroyed. Bladetusk Boar (mana value 4 > 2) survives.

actions.description: Initial: p1 has 2x Path of Peril in hand; p2 has 2x Llanowar Elves and 2x Bladetusk Boar on battlefield; both players at 40 life
actions.note: p1 has {colorless:5, white:1, black:3} covering both casts. p2 controls 2x Llanowar Elves (mana value 1) and 2x Bladetusk Boar (mana value 4).
CR 601.2: p1 casts Path of Peril paying the normal mana cost {1}{B}{B}. No alt_cost — this is a normal cast. Condition::WasCleaved will be false at resolution.

actions.description: After casting: Path of Peril on stack; p1 hand down to 1 card
actions.note: Path of Peril is on the stack with was_cleaved=false.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack (Path of Peril) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.148a + 701.8a: was_cleaved=false, if_false branch executes: DestroyAll { creature, max_cmc: 2 }. Only Llanowar Elves (CMC 1) destroyed; Bladetusk Boar (CMC 4) spared per CR 202.3.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Path of Peril resolves. was_cleaved=false selects if_false (DestroyAll max_cmc:2). Two Llanowar Elves (mana value 1) are destroyed. Two Bladetusk Boars (mana value 4) survive because 4 > 2.
Llanowar Elves #1 (CMC 1 <= 2) destroyed by Path of Peril normal cast; moves to p2 graveyard.
Llanowar Elves #2 (CMC 1 <= 2) destroyed by Path of Peril normal cast; moves to p2 graveyard.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked. Only the two Llanowar Elves died. Bladetusk Boars (CMC 4 > 2) remain on battlefield per the max_cmc filter.

actions.description: After normal cast resolution: two Llanowar Elves destroyed in p2 graveyard; two Bladetusk Boars still on p2 battlefield; first Path of Peril in p1 graveyard; p1 has 1 Path of Peril in hand
actions.note: CR 702.148a + CR 202.3: Normal (non-cleaved) cast only destroyed creatures with mana value <= 2 (the two Llanowar Elves). Bladetusk Boar (mana value 4) correctly survives. PB-19 fix: path_of_peril.rs if_false branch now uses DestroyAll { max_cmc: 2 }.

script.step_note: Cast 2: p1 casts Path of Peril with the cleave cost ({4}{W}{B}) using cast_spell_cleave. Condition::WasCleaved is true — the if_true branch (destroy all creatures, no restriction) executes, destroying the remaining 2 Bladetusk Boars on p2 battlefield.

actions.description: Before cleave cast: p1 has 1 Path of Peril in hand; p2 has 2 Bladetusk Boars on battlefield (survived first cast); stack empty
actions.note: p1 still has the second Path of Peril. Remaining mana covers the cleave cost {4}{W}{B} (colorless:4, white:1, black:1). Two Bladetusk Boars remain from the first cast.
CR 702.148a: p1 pays the cleave cost {4}{W}{B} as an alternative cost (CR 118.9). was_cleaved=true — the if_true branch will select DestroyAll { creature } (no CMC restriction) at resolution.

actions.description: After cleave cast: Path of Peril on stack; p1 hand empty
actions.note: Second Path of Peril is on the stack with was_cleaved=true.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack (Path of Peril, cleaved) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.148a + 701.8a: was_cleaved=true, if_true branch executes: DestroyAll { creature } (no CMC restriction). Both Bladetusk Boars are destroyed.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Cleaved Path of Peril resolves. was_cleaved=true — Condition::WasCleaved selects if_true (all creatures, no CMC filter). Both Bladetusk Boars (CMC 4) are destroyed.
Bladetusk Boar #1 destroyed by cleaved Path of Peril; moves to p2 graveyard.
Bladetusk Boar #2 destroyed by cleaved Path of Peril; moves to p2 graveyard.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked. Both Bladetusk Boars destroyed by the cleaved cast.

actions.description: Final state: stack empty; hand empty; p1 graveyard has 2x Path of Peril; p2 graveyard has 2x Llanowar Elves and 2x Bladetusk Boar; both players at 40 life
actions.note: Both casts resolved correctly. Normal cast destroyed only creatures with CMC <= 2 (Llanowar Elves). Cleaved cast destroyed all remaining creatures (Bladetusk Boars). CR 702.148a fully validated: cleave cost changes the effect scope from a CMC-restricted filter to an unrestricted all-creatures destroy.
